Name: Newgrange
Description: A prehistoric Neolithic monument from the Stone Age in the Boyne Valley constructed around 5.200 years ago and a World Heritage site

Name: Knowth
Description: Megalithic passage tombs consisting of one lage mound and 18 smaller mounds constructed around 5000 years ago

Name: Dowth
Description: Megalithic passage tombs consisting of one mound surrounded by kerbstones

Name: Carrowmore megalithic cemetery
Description: Irish oldest and densest place of Neolithic tombs guarding ancient history and rituals

Name: Céide Fields
Description: The oldest stone-walled fields that overlook the Atlantic Ocena up against the cliffs below

Name: Dún Aonghasa
Description: The largest prehistoric stone fort on the Aran Islands

Name: Drombeg Stone Circle
Description: A circle of 17 standing stones with an urn burial in the centre

Name: Uragh Stone Circle
Description: A circle of two portal stones
column6: two side stones and one recumbent stone
